use_cases.title: Webhook delivery rebuilt with retry-with-jitter and a DLQ
use_cases.problem: Synchronous webhook dispatch from core business logic coupled producer availability to every consumer. Recovered endpoints were getting hammered by synchronised retry storms; events were being lost; consumers were silently double-processing duplicates.
use_cases.intervention: Queue-backed dispatcher (SQS / RabbitMQ / Kafka), exponential backoff with random jitter on retries, dead-letter queue for unprocessable events, V4 UUID event IDs, HMAC-signed payloads, dedup table at the consumer with UNIQUE constraint on event_id.
use_cases.outcome_quantified: Synchronised retry spikes drop by >80%; lost events approach zero; duplicate-processing class eliminated at the consumer.

use_cases.title: KYC API integration cuts onboarding from 24 hours to minutes
use_cases.problem: Manual KYC review queues were a 24-hour customer-journey choke point; document handoffs, sanctions screening, and liveness verification were stitched together with email and spreadsheets.
use_cases.intervention: API-led KYC orchestration: document capture, liveness checks, sanctions screening, watchlist matching, and decisioning behind a contract-first OpenAPI surface; idempotency on submission; OpenTelemetry per stage.
use_cases.outcome_quantified: 90% onboarding-time reduction (24h -> minutes); 40-60% cost savings on KYC ops; 50% reduction in fraudulent account registrations (documented industry cases).

use_cases.title: Payment-gateway switch with zero double-charges via idempotency keys
use_cases.problem: PSP migration risked duplicate charges, chargebacks, and customer-trust erosion. Without idempotency keys, network timeouts triggered client-side retries that processed twice.
use_cases.intervention: Client-generated V4 UUID idempotency keys on every charge / refund / capture; 24-hour key TTL; exponential backoff on retries; signed webhook verification on raw body; reconciliation worker proving the ledger matches the gateway daily.
use_cases.outcome_quantified: <2ms overhead per request (Stripe's documented baseline); duplicate-charge class eliminated; weekly finance-grade reconciliation report.
use_cases.named_pattern: idempotency keys + signed webhooks + reconciliation worker

use_cases.title: PayShap real-time payment integration with SARB-grade reliability
use_cases.problem: PayShap is the SARB-led real-time rail; participant integration requires ISO 20022 message handling, idempotency on credit-transfer instructions, and resilience that meets banking-grade RTOs.
use_cases.intervention: ISO 20022 message adapters, idempotency keys per instruction, circuit breakers around BankservAfrica calls, OpenTelemetry tracing per message, runbook for SARB incident reporting (24h notification, 48h written report).
use_cases.outcome_quantified: Meets SARB Directive 1 of 2024: 2-hour RTO for critical systems (max 8h); incident reporting workflow tested.
use_cases.named_pattern: ISO 20022 + idempotency + circuit breakers + SARB-grade runbook

use_cases.title: Partner onboarding cut from 8 weeks to ~1 week with self-service developer portal
use_cases.problem: Manual partner onboarding consumed weeks of integration-engineering time per partner; documentation drift caused integration breakage on each release.
use_cases.intervention: Self-service developer portal, contract-first OpenAPI specs, generated mock servers and SDKs, contract tests in CI, sandbox credentials, automated provisioning.
use_cases.outcome_quantified: Industry case: 8 weeks -> ~1 week; one fintech got 14 days -> 3 hours via Swagger-based docs; centralised docs cut onboarding by 75% (Postman 2023 SOTI).
use_cases.named_pattern: contract-first OpenAPI + developer portal + automated provisioning

use_cases.title: Replace brittle ERP-CRM point-to-point integrations with contract-first integration layer
use_cases.problem: SA mid-market enterprise running 10+ third-party systems on hand-rolled connectors; every vendor schema change broke production; no consistent error handling or observability.
use_cases.intervention: Typed integration layer the client owns: anti-corruption layer at each vendor boundary, versioned OpenAPI + AsyncAPI contracts, Kafka or Redis Streams event bus, OpenTelemetry instrumentation, Terraform for infrastructure.
use_cases.outcome_quantified: Vendor schema changes become localised refactors instead of multi-day fire drills; integration TCO maintenance share (30-40% of cost) materially reduced.
use_cases.named_pattern: contract-first integration layer + anti-corruption + event bus

use_cases.title: PCI-DSS 4.0 API gateway hardening (Req 6.5.6 + Req 6.3.2)
use_cases.problem: Bank / fintech preparing for PCI-DSS 4.0 (effective 1 April 2025) needed explicit API security controls: BOLA mitigation, broken function-level authorisation checks, data-exposure scans, full request/response logging, component inventory.
use_cases.intervention: API gateway-level enforcement of object-level authorisation, rate limits, input validation, signed-payload verification; bespoke + third-party component inventory; continuous patch management; mTLS at sensitive edges.
use_cases.outcome_quantified: Closes Req 6.5.6 / Req 6.3.2 gaps before assessment; addresses OWASP API #1 (BOLA, present in ~40% of attacks).
use_cases.named_pattern: PCI-DSS 4.0 + OWASP API Top 10 + mTLS at gateway

use_cases.title: Circuit breakers around third-party PSP calls
use_cases.problem: PSP latency spikes during peak load were cascading into the merchant's checkout: slow downstream calls exhausted the thread pool; entire merchant service degraded; p99 latency on partner calls spiked uncontrollably.
use_cases.intervention: Resilience4j-style circuit breaker (Closed / Open / Half-Open states) on outbound PSP calls; bulkheads to isolate slow downstreams; layered retry + rate limiter; structured logs with correlation IDs.
use_cases.outcome_quantified: Tail-latency on partner calls becomes survivable; cascading failure prevented; mean-time-to-diagnosis drops sharply.

use_cases.title: OpenTelemetry distributed tracing across the API estate
use_cases.problem: p99 latency spikes diagnosed by guesswork; no causal chain across services A->B->C; observability bolted on after incidents instead of designed in.
use_cases.intervention: OpenTelemetry traces + metrics + logs from day one; histogram bucket boundaries set to SLO targets (e.g., 500ms for p99); correlation IDs propagated end-to-end; per-route latency + error-rate dashboards.
use_cases.outcome_quantified: Symptom-to-cause time on incidents drops from hours of guessing to a single trace; SLO compliance becomes measurable, not aspirational.

use_cases.title: Stitch / Capitec Pay / Absa Pay / Nedbank Direct EFT integration
use_cases.problem: Enterprise SA merchants (Takealot, Mr D, MTN, Vodacom, TFG's Bash) are moving beyond card-only to direct-bank rails; integration scope spans pay-by-bank, debit order, DebiCheck, wallets, and reconciliation.
use_cases.intervention: Contract-first integration to Stitch (or direct to Capitec Pay / Absa Pay / Nedbank Direct EFT); idempotency keys; signed webhook verification; reconciliation worker; OpenTelemetry per gateway.
use_cases.outcome_quantified: End-to-end card + direct-bank product surface with daily reconciliation; named precedent: Stitch serves the enterprise SA merchant list cited.

evidence_strength.note: SA-specific transaction-volume figures for individual gateways (PayFast/Yoco/Peach FY2025) were not surfaced in publicly available sources at retrieval time. Use cases that depend on those numbers are labelled evidence: pattern with industry-source citations rather than client-specific case studies. The Stitch customer-list use case (UC-API-10) is evidence: case-study because the named enterprise customers are published on Stitch's site and in TechCabal coverage.
